about.name: Kamilla Poriter
about.description.1: I find joy in creating beautiful, accessible, and performant web interfaces. Relying on design sensibility and modern technologies, I transform ideas into elegant digital solutions (even from forgotten code).
about.description.2: Driven by curiosity and a commitment to continuous learning. I enjoy challenges that make me try new things and hone my craft.
about.highlight.4: ♿ Advocate for transparency and accessibility in web development
education.title: Education
education.items.0.description: Specialized in software engineering with focus on software development. Emphasis on modern web technologies, algorithms, and UX design.
education.items.0.achievement.1: Main interests were applied mathematics, algorithms, drafting and modeling
education.items.0.achievement.2: Interests included web development, UX design and databases (which goes perfectly with the previous point)
education.items.0.achievement.3: Nevertheless, I defended my thesis on the topic of logistics and management of an abstract library in C++
education.items.1.description: Yes, I did a Java developer internship! Although it was my first experience in a corporate environment, I quickly adapted and realized that my true calling is JS
education.items.1.achievement.2: Mainly worked with maintaining and fixing bugs in existing Java applications and testing
education.items.1.achievement.3: Was offered to switch to frontend development as I showed interest in JS during college studies and could give valuable advice on improvements
education.items.2.description: Intensive Java development course. Every day a new task and project to practice Java programming skills
education.items.2.achievement.2: Successfully completed and received a certificate of completion and the opportunity to do an internship at Accenture
education.items.3.description: Advanced mathematics and computer science. Strong foundation in STEM and critical thinking
experience.title: Experience
experience.subtitle: A journey through professional growth and impactful projects
experience.items.0.title: Software Programmer
experience.items.0.description: Remote work as a frontend developer in an international company on a distributed team (without fixation on a specific project subdivision). Gained experience in the importance of complex system architecture, clear development processes and structure.
experience.items.0.highlight.1: Remote work as a frontend developer in an international company on a distributed team (without fixation on a specific project subdivision)
experience.items.0.highlight.2: Gained experience in the architecture of complex interconnected systems, improving the clarity of development processes, structuring and improving collaboration in global companies
experience.items.0.highlight.3: Faced problems due to the scale of the project, constant project switching and strong time zone differences
experience.items.0.highlight.4: The experience clarified my priorities as an engineer
experience.items.0.highlight.5: Reinforced understanding of the engineering culture in which I thrive: thoughtful design, aligned teams, and sustainable workflows

experience.items.1.highlight.7: Collaborated with cross-functional teams to implement solutions meeting business objectives

funfacts.subtitle: Random Daily Fact
funfacts.sun.title: Sun Facts
funfacts.moon.title: Moon Facts
funfacts.sun.fallback.1: The Sun contains 99.86% of the mass in the Solar System.
funfacts.sun.fallback.2: Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.
funfacts.sun.fallback.3: The Sun is a nearly perfect sphere of hot plasma — mostly hydrogen and helium.
funfacts.sun.fallback.4: Sunspots are cooler, darker regions caused by magnetic activity.
funfacts.sun.fallback.5: The Sun will evolve into a red giant in about 5 billion years.
funfacts.moon.fallback.1: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite.
funfacts.moon.fallback.2: The Moon's gravitational influence produces Earth's ocean tides.
funfacts.moon.fallback.3: The same side of the Moon always faces Earth due to tidal locking.
funfacts.moon.fallback.4: The Moon has no atmosphere, so there's no weather or wind.
funfacts.moon.fallback.5: The Moon is gradually moving away from Earth at about 3.8 cm per year.
